dependencies.description: Hatch plugin for versioning with your preferred VCS
dependencies.name: py-hatch-vcs

dependencies.description:
A lightweight dependency injection and result processing framework for
Python using type hints. Emphasis is on simplicity, ease of use, and
minimal impact on source code.
dependencies.name: py-in-n-out

dependencies.description: The PyPA recommended tool for installing Python packages.
dependencies.name: py-pip

dependencies.description: Fast python callback/event system modeled after Qt Signals.
dependencies.name: py-psygnal

dependencies.description: Data validation and settings management using Python type hinting.
dependencies.name: py-pydantic

dependencies.description:
This package provides (unofficial) compatibility mixins and function
adaptors for pydantic v1-v2 cross compatibility. It allows you to use
either v1 or v2 API names, regardless of the pydantic version installed.
(Prefer using v2 names when possible).
dependencies.name: py-pydantic-compat

dependencies.description: Missing widgets and components for PyQt/PySide
dependencies.name: py-superqt

dependencies.description:
The typing_extensions module contains both backports of these changes as
well as experimental types that will eventually be added to the typing
module, such as Protocol (see PEP 544 for details about protocols and
static duck typing).
dependencies.name: py-typing-extensions

dependencies.description: A Spack managed Python virtual environment
dependencies.name: python-venv

dependent_to.description:
napari is a fast, interactive, multi-dimensional image viewer for
Python. It's designed for browsing, annotating, and analyzing large
multi-dimensional images. It's built on top of Qt (for the GUI), vispy
(for performant GPU-based rendering), and the scientific Python stack
(numpy, scipy).
dependent_to.name: py-napari

description:
app-model is a generic application schema implemented in python, for
declarative organization of application data, such as menus,
keybindings, actions/commands, etc...
name: py-app-model

variants.description: Build systems supported by the package
variants.name: build_system
